Target show rate achievable via lead nurture is 70%

numbers.axis: call show rate and resulting business impact

anchors.region: target show rate with lead nurture playbook

anchors.value: ~40% more calls attended without other changes

claims.text: Alex Hormozi explicitly rejects the suggestion that a lower-ticket offer should be introduced to convert more of the existing 20,000-lead monthly audience, on the grounds that the business is already printing money at high margins and the constraint is operational (DM setter capacity), not price sensitivity.
conditions: Business is already at 80% margins with $300K/month revenue
Existing offer is $3K and $9K (expensive relative to Argentinian market)
Lead volume is high (20,000+/month) but conversion is low due to operational bottleneck, not price
claims.mechanism: Lowering ticket price dilutes margin without solving the actual constraint; the 0.3% conversion rate is a setter-capacity problem, not a price objection problem.
bounded_by: Advice is specific to a business already generating strong margin; a different calculus applies when margins are thin

claims.text: Alex Hormozi estimates that this business could plausibly reach 1% lead-to-client conversion rate (from a current 0.3%) by adequately staffing the DM setter function, without changes to the offer, price, or sales process.
conditions: 20,000 leads/month baseline
Constraint is DM setter headcount, not offer quality or qualification rigor
Lead source is Instagram organic DM funnel
claims.mechanism: Increasing DM setter headcount 3-4x allows the team to actually work all incoming leads rather than leaving the majority uncontacted; worked leads convert at a measurable rate, unworked leads convert at zero.

numbers.axis: lead-to-client conversion rate

claims.text: Alex Hormozi treats a 68% qualification rate among call-show attendees as acceptable and not the binding constraint, distinguishing between metrics that could be better versus metrics that are the actual growth bottleneck.
conditions: Closers have spare capacity
DM setter capacity is the confirmed binding constraint
Qualification rate of 68% among showed calls

numbers.axis: qualification rate among call-show attendees

bounded_by: Assessment would change if DM setter capacity were resolved and qualification rate became the next binding constraint

claims.text: Alex Hormozi states that in a luxury service business, winning requires maximizing volume of dollars per transaction rather than volume of transactions, because the fixed positioning and delivery cost is the same whether the price is $30K or $250K.
conditions: luxury-positioned service with high fixed delivery cost
market size is inherently limited by the ultra-high-net-worth wedding segment
claims.mechanism: Luxury positioning limits total addressable volume by definition; the only lever to grow revenue is price per transaction, not transaction count.
bounded_by: Does not apply before luxury positioning is credibly established
Does not apply to mass-market services where volume scaling is feasible

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends that the video sales letter (VSL) sent before a luxury sales call should open with a frame about where wedding memories actually come from, arguing that entertainment drives memory more than flowers or catering, and therefore deserves a proportionally larger budget allocation.
conditions: luxury wedding entertainment sales context
prospect has already opted in and is pre-qualified
claims.mechanism: Framing entertainment as the highest memory-contribution category (versus flowers, food, decor) shifts the buyer's mental accounting from comparing entertainment cost to absolute price, to comparing entertainment cost relative to total wedding spend and to the memory it creates. This reframe makes a $100K entertainment price feel proportionate when flowers alone cost $300K-$500K.

numbers.axis: example wedding spending on categories

bounded_by: Requires credible data or survey evidence about wedding memory contribution by category to survive scrutiny

claims.text: Alex Hormozi states that embedding a secret word or compliance check inside the VSL (e.g., asking the prospect to text back a piece of information from the video) is the mechanism to verify and enforce pre-call viewing, because prospects commit to watching by saying they will, and the secret word surfaces whether they actually did.
conditions: VSL used to pre-frame prospects before a sales call
prospects are given the option to watch alone vs. together
claims.mechanism: Offering prospects the choice to watch on their own generates a verbal commitment to watch. The secret word (e.g., ask for head count or a specific detail from the video) serves as a compliance check; if the prospect texts it back, they watched. If they did not, the sales rep has a non-confrontational script to re-engage before the call.

claims.topic: Owner Delivery Time as Scaling Constraint
claims.text: For a med-spa owner at $3M revenue generating $700K net profit (plus ~$200K medical director salary = ~$1M total owner earnings), the binding constraint on scaling to multiple locations is not money and not sales volume, but the owner's personal clinical time. Alex Hormozi diagnosed this by noting that 80% of the owner's time is spent practicing as a physician, leaving 20% for business operations.
conditions: Business already generating ~30% seller discretionary earnings margins
Owner wants to scale to multiple locations
Service business where owner is licensed practitioner (physician)
claims.mechanism: When the founder is the primary clinical delivery vehicle, every hour spent treating patients is an hour not spent on the business development, hiring, and operational work required to open and run additional locations.

numbers.axis: Owner time allocation

bounded_by: Does not apply when the business is below ~30% margins and cannot afford to hire a replacement
Does not apply when the owner is not also the primary clinical provider

claims.topic: Hiring Peer Clinician as Multi-Location Prerequisite
claims.text: Alex Hormozi asserts that for a healthcare/professional-services business where the owner is the licensed provider, hiring a peer clinician (at approximately the owner's medical director salary) is the prerequisite step to multi-location scaling because it is the only way to free founder time for business expansion. Without this hire, all growth attempts hit the same time ceiling regardless of capital or sales improvements.
conditions: Business has sufficient profit (~$700K+) to afford the hire
Healthcare or professional-services business where owner holds the professional license
Owner wants to scale beyond one location
claims.mechanism: The owner's time is the bottleneck resource; a peer clinician absorbs the clinical delivery load and frees the founder for the operational bandwidth required to scale. The hire is self-funding because the owner's medical director salary (~$200K) is redirected to the new hire, leaving the $700K net profit intact.

numbers.axis: Hire cost vs. owner medical director salary

anchors.region: owner medical director draw being redirected

bounded_by: Does not apply if the business is too thin (margins too low) to afford the hire
Does not apply to non-licensed service businesses where the owner is not the bottleneck clinician
